content.problemStatement:
There is a grid containing NNN rows and NNN columns. The cell at the ithi^{th}ith row from the top and jthj^{th}jth column from the left is a passable space if Si,jS_{i,j}Si,j​ is `'.'` and a blocked cell if Si,jS_{i,j}Si,j​ is `'#'`.

Chef is at the top-left cell and wants to reach the bottom-right cell. Chef can move one cell down, or right to a passable cell. He can't leave the grid. If Chef is at the cell (i,j)(i,j)(i,j), he can collect Ai,jA_{i,j}Ai,j​ coins.

Now, Chef has a special ability using which he can make all the blocked cells in a path of length KKK into passable cells. Chef uses this ability before moving from the top-left cell. It is possible that **some of the cells in the chosen KKK length path are already passable**. More formally, Chef can choose a sequence of KKK cells such that for each iii (1≤i<K)(1 \leq i \lt K)(1≤i<K), the ithi^{th}ith and (i+1)th(i+1)^{th}(i+1)th cells of the sequence are **adjacent** and make all these KKK cells passable.

Find the maximum amount of coins Chef can collect in the path from the top-left cell to the bottom-right cell. Print −1-1−1 if it is impossible to reach the bottom-right cell.

**Note**: Two cells are considered to be adjacent if they share a common side. The cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) has four adjacent cells i.e. the cells (i−1,j)(i - 1, j)(i−1,j), (i+1,j)(i + 1, j)(i+1,j), (i,j−1)(i, j - 1)(i,j−1) and (i,j+1)(i, j + 1)(i,j+1). It is guaranteed that the top-left cell of the grid is a passable cell.


Input Format
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.



The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N,KN, KN,K.
NNN  lines follow. The ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains a string SiS_iSi​ of length NNN. The jthj^{th}jth character of SiS_iSi​ is `'.'` if the cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) is passable, or `'#'` if it is a blocked cell.
Next NNN lines follow. The ithi^{th}ith of these lines contains NNN space-separated integers  Ai,jA_{i,j}Ai,j​, denoting the number of coins in the cell (i,j)(i, j)(i,j).


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the maximum amount of coin Chef can collect in the path or −1-1−1 if it is impossible to reach the bottom-right cell.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the top-left cell of the grid is a passable cell.
Sum N2N^2N2 over all testcases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef breaks the blocked cell (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1) and choose the path -
  (1,1)→(2,1)→(2,2)\;(1, 1) \rightarrow (2, 1) \rightarrow (2,2)(1,1)→(2,1)→(2,2). Total coins collected in the path is 1+3+4=81 + 3 + 4 = 81+3+4=8.
Test case 222: There is no way to choose a path of length 333 such that after breaking the blocked cells in the path, it is possible to reach the bottom-right cell.
Test case 333: Chef chooses the path of consisting of cells (2,3),(2, 3),(2,3), (2,4)(2, 4)(2,4), (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4), (4,4)(4, 4)(4,4) and break the blocked cells in this path. Then Chef chooses the path - (1,1)→(1,2)→(1,3)→(2,3)→(2,4)→(3,4)→(4,4)→(5,4)(1, 1) \rightarrow (1, 2) \rightarrow (1,3) \rightarrow (2, 3) \rightarrow (2, 4) \rightarrow (3, 4) \rightarrow (4, 4) \rightarrow (5, 4)(1,1)→(1,2)→(1,3)→(2,3)→(2,4)→(3,4)→(4,4)→(5,4)   →(5,5)\;\rightarrow (5, 5)→(5,5). Total coins collected in the path is 2+3+42 + 3 + 42+3+4   +15+12+8\;+ 15 + 12 + 8+15+12+8   +9+4+5=62\;+ 9 + 4 + 5  = 62+9+4+5=62.
